eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to MySQL Minimum Correctness Configuration (single-node standalone)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Representative benchmark-level estimate for a standalone MySQL/InnoDB deployment used as technology profile baseline. Real-world performance can vary significantly depending on workload, hardware, tuning, and concurrency settings.
ps_throughput.source: MySQL 8.0 Reference Manual – InnoDB transactional engine and standalone server architecture

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: MySQL architecture – absence of protocol-level transaction fees

eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for MySQL MCC

parallel_execution.motivation: In the adopted standalone InnoDB baseline, transaction processing is not structurally designed around parallel execution across multiple independent execution domains; scalability mainly relies on vertical scaling of a single server.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The adopted MCC excludes sharding, clustering, and distributed partitioning, so no native horizontal workload partitioning is assumed in the benchmark baseline.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: The standalone MySQL deployment processes commits within one database instance rather than across multiple independent ordering or execution domains.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Within a single-node relational engine, transaction coordination overhead is comparatively limited because no distributed consensus or cross-node coordination is required in the adopted baseline.

ps_scalability.source: MySQL standalone InnoDB architecture and benchmark baseline assumptions

append_only_structural_history.motivation: MySQL tables are mutable and do not enforce append-only semantics.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: No native cryptographic chaining of records is implemented.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Authentication exists at user level, not as cryptographic transaction signing on each record write.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: No trustless independent verification model exists in the native MySQL architecture.

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Committed transactions are durable under ACID guarantees in the native database model.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: No multi-node or multi-party confirmation exists in the standalone native configuration.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: After commit, rollback is not part of the normal transaction protocol at application level.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: A standalone MySQL deployment depends on a single server receiving writes.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: No quorum-based write continuation exists in the native standalone model.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: No automatic failover exists in the native standalone configuration.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: No multi-node write survivability is structurally provided in the MCC or native standalone model.

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: Database control is centralized under a single administrative authority.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: No distributed or multi-entity validation model exists.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: There is no open or independently governed validator admission model.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The architecture does not define a distributed validator set at all.

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: MySQL supports fine-grained privileges and roles.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: User and role lifecycle can be managed within the system.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Identity governance is structurally centralized in the database administrator/operator.

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: A single privileged actor can compromise the system.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: No majority threshold exists for compromise.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: No supermajority threshold exists for compromise.
